title: Add repo-native workflow observability helpers
summary: Add lightweight read-only scripts that render HumbleStudio ticket, lane, status, git, and worktree state directly from repo-owned workflow files so day-to-day operation matches the nearby HumbleControl and HumbleSudoku flow more closely.
acceptance_criteria: HumbleStudio exposes a read-only ticket board renderer for HS tickets and lane occupancy.
HumbleStudio exposes a read-only status renderer that combines repo status truth with live git and worktree context.
Local docs mention the new supported workflow commands instead of leaving them discoverable only through Scripts inspection.
Validation and command evidence prove the new helpers run successfully in the current repository state.

evidence.note: This slice was selected after comparing HumbleStudio workflow surfaces to HumbleControl and HumbleSudoku, which already expose practical read-only ticket and status helpers.
Rendered a compact ticket and lane board from the local HS workflow files.
Rendered repo-owned status together with live git and worktree context.
Validated both HS workflow tickets after adding the new observability slice.
All workflow helper scripts compiled successfully.
Whitespace and patch hygiene checks passed for the observability slice.

trigger.source_ref: Follow-up workflow parity slice after canonical contract onboarding, inspired by HumbleControl and HumbleSudoku.

agent_notes: This slice intentionally stays read-only and does not claim lanes, allocate tickets, or mutate git state.
If the workflow becomes routine, the next safe step is a narrowly scoped lane-claim or ticket-bootstrap helper.
